parsed_review.summary: CP00-430 (risk A, 10 units RP14.P02.M04.S03-S12, Secondary Workflow Slice) is a descriptor-only settlement-core pack and its boundaries are well-supported and internally consistent. Scope, deliverable distribution (implementation 6 / security_audit 2 / ui 2), and micro-title distribution match across review-baseline, manifest, closeout-pack-plan, registry pack-binding, contract (current_pack, pack_binding, cp430_requirements, p02_workflow_slice_descriptor, historical_pack_bindings), tests, and the validate script. Every workflow-slice row is descriptor_only/runtime_execution:false/customer_safe_errors_only:true/raw_payload_included:false, verified in service.js generation, contract JSON, and validators.js. The product source (registry/service/validators/index) is self-contained descriptor logic with no fs/network/DB access; no-write/no-real-data attestations are comprehensive and consistent. The pack-level production_ready claim is false (runtime_ready:false, implementation_layer:descriptor) and is NOT overclaimed; production_ready_candidate:true is only a Hermes descriptor candidate flag. Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false. Hermes packet is deterministic review/evidence descriptor data (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false), not product runtime. Readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1436-byte valid JSON; no auth-failure/0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped evidence). The handoff chain is gap-free (CP429 to S02, CP430 S03-S12, CP431 from S13). No P0/P1/P2 issues found. Two P3 informational notes only. This review is by a read-only independent reviewer and makes no final-approval or enterprise-trust claim; human final approval remains required and is closed outside this receipt.

findings.title: Post-review evidence artifacts not yet present (expected at this hardening stage)
findings.details: manifest required_pack_artifacts and evidence_refs list docs/closeout-packs/cp00-430/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but only manifest.json and command-evidence.json currently exist in the pack directory. This is consistent with pack_level_claude_review.status="pending", valid_review_receipts=[], and the hardening_sequence: these three artifacts are produced by the downstream steps (normalize, receipt_validate, finding_adjudication, construction_inspection) that run after this read-only review. It is not a defect in the implementation under review.
findings.recommendation: Ensure the post-review pipeline generates and validates claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, and that npm run closeout-pack:validate confirms all required_pack_artifacts exist before commit/closeout.

findings.title: Cumulative no_write_attestation inherits prior packs' validates_* flags
findings.details: SETTLEMENT_CORE_CP430_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION spreads SETTLEMENT_CORE_CP429_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION, so CP430's attestation also carries validates_scope_contract_foundation_descriptor_only, validates_p01_closeout_p02_foundation_descriptor_only, validates_p02_contract_implementation_slice_descriptor_only, and validates_p02_implementation_workflow_slice_descriptor_only in addition to validates_p02_workflow_slice_descriptor_only. Semantically broad for a workflow-slice pack, but this is intentional cumulative design and is fully consistent between the registry constant and the contract JSON (the validate script deepEquals them). No write or runtime behavior is implied; all dispatch/write flags remain false.
findings.recommendation: No action required. If tighter per-pack semantics are ever desired, scope the validates_* flags to the current pack; non-blocking and must remain consistent with the contract + validate script if changed.
